title: anthocephalus macrophyllus
intro: Anthocephalus macrophyllus, also known as the Kadam tree, is a deciduous tree native to Asia and widely distributed in Southeast Asia, particularly in India, Sri Lanka, and parts of Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Anthocephalus macrophyllus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat various types of infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it is also used in Ayurvedic medicine to prepare various formulations, such as decoctions and capsules.
